blockers.description: It is unclear what a "low-performing school in SAT" is, which could refer to average math scores, reading scores, or excellence rate.
example_questions: What does "low-performing in SAT" mean?
How do I determine if a school is low-performing in SAT?
Which SAT metric should I use to identify low-performing schools?
Does low-performing refer to math scores, reading scores, or something else?
blockers.resolution: A low-performing school is one where the average SAT math score is less than 550.

blockers.description: The term "small schools"  is vague and does not specify a threshold.
example_questions: What qualifies as a small school?
What enrollment threshold defines a small school?
How many students must a school have to be considered small?
Which enrollment column should I use to determine school size?
blockers.resolution: A small school is one with an enrollment in grade 12 (enroll12) of a maximum of 30 students.

blockers.description: The term "recently opened" does not specify a date range or cutoff for when a school must have opened.
example_questions: What date range qualifies as "recently opened"?
How recent must a school's opening date be?
What is the cutoff date for a school to be considered recently opened?
Does "recently opened" mean within the last 5 years, 10 years, or something else?
blockers.resolution: A recently opened school is one with an OpenDate on or after January 1, 1990.

blockers.description: The term "academic readiness index" is not defined anywhere in the business information or database schema.
example_questions: What is the academic readiness index?
How is academic readiness index calculated?
What columns are used to compute the academic readiness index?
What threshold makes an academic readiness index considered "low"?
blockers.resolution: The academic readiness index is calculated as the number of SAT test takers (NumTstTkr) divided by the 12th-grade enrollment (enroll12). A "low" academic readiness index is lower than 0.8.

blockers.description: The virtual column contains single-letter codes (F, V, C, N, P), but their meanings are not explained in the column description.
example_questions: What do the values in the virtual column represent?
Which value in the virtual column indicates an exclusively virtual school?
What does the "F" mean in the virtual column?
How do I identify exclusively virtual schools using the Virtual column?
blockers.resolution: A school that is exclusively virtual has a value of "F" in the virtual column.
